reasoning: The Lean formalization correctly captures both claims of the informal statement. The hypothesis `hABC` encodes A, B, C on the unit circle with equal pairwise distances and the added `dist A B ≠ 0` surfaces the implicit premise that the three points are distinct (an equilateral triangle is implied by "equidistant points"). The first claim (a, b, c form a triangle) is captured by the existence of X, Y, Z with matching pairwise distances in ℝ² — this existence is equivalent to the triangle inequality holding. The second claim (area depends only on |OP|) is correctly captured by placing `∃ f : ℝ → ℝ` OUTSIDE the `∀ P`, so a single function f ties all areas to |OP|. The volume of `convexHull ℝ {X, Y, Z}` in EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 2) is the area of the triangle. The strict inequality `dist 0 P < 1` correctly encodes "in the circle's interior". The side-length mapping (XY=PA, YZ=PB, XZ=PC) is a valid assignment of the three sides.
